title: Kafka
objectives: Explain topics, partitions, offsets and consumer groups
Reason about ordering and delivery guarantees

sections.markdown: Kafka is a distributed, append-only **log**. Producers write records to a **topic** split across **partitions**; consumers read via **consumer groups** at their own pace by tracking **offsets**. Records are retained by time/size, not until consumed.

sections.code:
topic 'orders' with 3 partitions:
  P0: [r0 r1 r2 r3 ...]  offset per consumer group
  P1: [r0 r1 r2 r3 ...]
  P2: [r0 r1 r2 r3 ...]
Producer records key=userId → same partition → per-user ordering
Consumer group 'billing' has 3 consumers → each owns 1 partition

rows: One record → one consumer in the group
Time/size based; records don't disappear on read
Copies of each partition on brokers

sections.markdown: Ordering is guaranteed **per partition**, not per topic. Choose your partition key so related records land on the same partition (e.g. `userId`). More consumers than partitions = idle consumers; fewer = fanned-out but not maxed.
Committing offsets before processing succeeds — you'll skip records on crash. Commit AFTER successful processing to get at-least-once; use idempotent processing to handle inevitable duplicates.

Use compacted topics for state (latest per key); use tiered storage for long retention. For exactly-once, use Kafka's transactional producer + read-committed consumer. Monitor consumer lag as a top-line SLI.

flashcards.back: Per-partition ordering — records with the same key land on the same partition and stay ordered there.
Kafka assigns partitions to consumers in the group; one partition → one consumer per group at a time.
At-least-once by default; exactly-once needs transactional producer + read-committed consumer.

quiz.explain: Partitions cap parallelism inside a group.

quiz.q: For durable state per key, use…
quiz.explain: Compaction retains the latest record per key.

challenges.text: Produce and consume messages on a topic with 3 partitions.
Design a partition key for an orders topic with per-user ordering.
Implement at-least-once with idempotent DB upserts.
Enable exactly-once with transactional producer + read-committed consumer.

interviewQuestions: At-least-once vs exactly-once — how do you get each?
How do you monitor Kafka health? (lag, ISR)
